techcrunch.title: Why Apple’s slow-and-steady AI bet is starting to look pretty smart
techcrunch.summary: Can Apple's new AI glow up put to bed accusations that it's losing an all-important industry race?

techcrunch.title: Mercor’s Brendan Foody calls out Sequoia, accusing it of ‘dual-pricing’ valuation tricks
techcrunch.summary: Sequoia is just one of the top firms that sells same equity at two different prices.

techcrunch.title: As OpenAI files for IPO, Sam Altman’s eye-scanning company is doing layoffs, report says
techcrunch.summary: Tools for Humanity, Sam Altman's identity verification company, is reportedly struggling to generate revenue and will downsize its staff.

techcrunch.title: Apple’s WWDC AI demos looked more real after $250M false ad settlement
techcrunch.summary: The vibe of Apple's 2026 WWDC keynote felt like a spouse proudly listing all the honey-do-list items tackled. One subtle example: the many AI demos of someone standing, phone in hand.

techcrunch.title: OpenAI files confidentially for IPO, following Anthropic
techcrunch.summary: The filing comes a little more than a week after its main rival, Anthropic, also filed to go public, ramping up the race between the two AI firms.

techcrunch.title: Apple plays catch-up at WWDC
techcrunch.summary: Apple spent much of its WWDC keynote highlighting fixes, performance improvements, and long-requested features before unveiling its upgraded AI-powered Siri, signaling that the company wants users to see AI as just one part of a broader effort to improve its software.

techcrunch.title: Apple bets cheaper AI will woo small developers
techcrunch.summary: As AI experimentation grows more expensive, Apple is waiving cloud API costs for developers with fewer than 2 million first-time App Store downloads.

techcrunch.title: WWDC 2026: Everything announced on Siri AI, iOS 27, Apple Intelligence and more
techcrunch.summary: Apple primarily made the case for an improved experience with its longstanding Siri assistant, which like most other announcements had a hefty helping of AI.

techcrunch.title: Apple just taught your iPhone to finish your sentences, your photos, and your workflows
techcrunch.summary: Apple is adding new AI-powered features to Safari, Shortcuts, and Password apps.

techcrunch.title: Apple will let you build workflows using AI in its new Shortcuts app
techcrunch.summary: Shortcuts gets an AI upgrade, letting you describe the workflow you want in a prompt.
